name: Foundation/cors
description: CORS preflight conformance tests ported from Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E Rest/CorsTests.cs. Sends an OPTIONS preflight request to the Patient endpoint with Origin/Access-Control-Request-Method/Access-Control-Request-Headers request headers and asserts the standard Access-Control-* response headers using the engine's headerField assert criteria (Ignixa.TestScript.Expressions.HeaderCriteria, JSON key 'headerField'), which reads response headers via a case-insensitive header dictionary. The two 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' echo checks are warningOnly because header-value matching in this engine uses ordinal (case-sensitive) string comparison, while HTTP header token comparison is case-insensitive and other FHIR servers' CORS middleware may normalize casing differently than what was sent.

test.name: OPTIONS preflight for Patient returns 204 with the appropriate CORS response headers
test.description: An OPTIONS request with Origin, Access-Control-Request-Method: PUT, and Access-Control-Request-Headers: authorization,content-type must return HTTP 204 No Content and echo back the allowed origin/methods/headers plus a max-age of 1440 seconds.

operation.description: Send a CORS preflight OPTIONS request to Patient with Origin and Access-Control-Request-* headers

assert.description: Preflight OPTIONS request must return HTTP 204 No Content
Access-Control-Allow-Origin must reflect the requesting origin
Access-Control-Allow-Methods must be present and include the requested method
Access-Control-Allow-Headers should include 'authorization' (warningOnly: engine's contains check is case-sensitive; header casing may vary by server)
Access-Control-Allow-Headers should include 'content-type' (warningOnly: engine's contains check is case-sensitive; header casing may vary by server)
